trivia_bullets.text: Cryptic Cruiser is an Eldrazi Processor from Battle for Zendikar (October 2, 2015), part of the large-scale return to Zendikar. The Eldrazi were divided into distinct factions aligned with each titan: Ulamog's Processors, Kozilek's Processors, and Eldrazi Drones. Type line verified via Scryfall API as 'Creature — Eldrazi Processor'.

The Processor mechanic (activation cost: put an opponent's exiled card into their graveyard) was rated 'Development: Problematic' on Mark Rosewater's Storm Scale due to its 'A + B design' dependency on Ingest creatures supplying exiles. The mechanic requires both Ingest and Processor cards to function, consuming substantial design space for limited payoff.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Storm Scale: Zendikar and Battle for Zendikar (2016)

trivia_bullets.text: "The seas are no less imperiled than the dry lands. This fight is mine as much as it is yours." — Kiora, to Gideon Jura. The oceanic setting of the art directly mirrors Kiora's domain as a merfolk planeswalker whose power is rooted in seas and sea creatures. The quote frames the BFZ Gatewatch storyline where Kiora joined the resistance against the Eldrazi.

EDHREC rank of 26,172 places Cryptic Cruiser outside the mainstream Commander card pool. Its activated ability ({2}{U} + exile-consumption to tap a creature) requires a functioning Ingest engine plus mana investment for a one-turn tap effect, which rarely justifies the infrastructure cost in non-dedicated Eldrazi builds.

related_cards.name: Mist Intruder

related_cards.name: Ruin Processor
related_cards.note: Fellow Eldrazi Processor in the same set; shares the exile-consumption design axis

related_cards.name: Blight Herder

related_cards.name: Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger
related_cards.note: The titan whose brood the Processors belong to thematically
